rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF ROBERTO T. REYNA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Diane Mijango'S Final Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Diane Mijango, whose resignation as co-conservator was approved by the Court's order filed on 05/25/2021, and Rebecca Valdespino as the remaining sole conservator, have not filed a joint final account. The final account must be approved before Diane Mijango can be removed from the bond as co-conservator/obligor and before a new bond can be issued to Rebecca Valdespino as sole conservator. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KLARA HOLZER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/21 for a possible appearance to determine if notice was adequate. (No earlier availability.) There are numerous other trust beneficiaries besides the beneficiaries of the tangible personal property. They and their last known addresses are required to be set forth in the petition so that the Court can determine if notice was proper. Also, "replacing" an accounting, even for a nominal amount, is an AMENDMENT, not a "supplement". [CRC 7.3.] (A supplement or amendment to a petition should stand on its own as though it were an extension of the pleading it supplements or amends, and should not quote and respond directly to issues raised by an Examiner.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LARRY J STEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] (The executor did not file Corrected or Supplemental I&As to correct his total on Attachment 1 of the Final I&A or to report recovered escheated assets.) Refunds for amounts paid during estate administration are not includable in the fee base. (The receipt from the SFPL is dated 8/24/2020, but the executor alleges that the donation was made "prior to decedent's death" without any specificity. How does the executor know this and why does the receipt not reflect the actual date of the donation? Rev. and Tax. Code Section 19513 was repealed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/22/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE INDIVIDUAL RETIREMENT ACCOUNT OF THEODORE GLAZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition To Enforce Ira Beneficiary Designation And For 1) Delcaratory Relief; 2) Breach Of Contract; Or Alternatively; 3) Reformation Of Ira Contract
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/21 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Some of the legal authority cited in the petition caption does not appear to apply or to exist. Prob. C. Sect. 5000 specifically appears to give Schwab the authority to transfer the property to the designated beneficiary it has on file. If this is in fact a breach of contract issue, a Civil complaint should have been filed. There is no Civil Code Section 1554. The petition caption is incomplete as the petitioner is seeking damages. [CRC 7.102.] If the petitioner intended to seek relief under Probate Code Section 850 (see Prob. C. Sect. 5003(c)), to claim that the assets held in this IRA account should be conveyed to him via a contract that can be specifically enforced, then notice was defective. All possible heirs of the decedent would also need to be identified and either receive personal service of notice or waive such notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 851.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/22/21. (The register of actions also reflects another response that was filed on 7/23/21. No courtesy copy of this one has been received yet by the assigned Examiner as of 8/30/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD FRANCIS CONWAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The original will was not filed until 08/16/2021. The first two publications of notice were made on 08/11/2021 and 08/12/2021. The notice contained the following statement required by Probate Code section 8100: "The petition requests the decedent's will and codicils, if any, be admitted to probate. The will and any codicils are available for examination in the file kept by the court." Although a copy of the will was attached to the petition and had been available for examination by the time the notice was first published, the original will was not available for examination until after the notice was published twice. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809.
